pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
Shared Task on

Identifying misogynistic videos in Dravidian languages

MMLow @ AACL-IJCNLP 2026

The Shared Task on Identifying Misogynistic Videos in Dravidian Languages aims to advance research in multimodal content analysis and online safety by developing automated systems capable of detecting misogynistic content in video data. Misogyny, expressed through hatred, prejudice, discrimination, objectification, or derogatory attitudes toward women, has become increasingly prevalent across social media and video-sharing platforms. Early detection of such harmful content is essential for creating safer and more inclusive digital spaces. In this shared task, participants will be provided with a curated collection of videos in two major Dravidian languages: Malayalam and Tamil. Each video may contain spoken content, visual cues, textual overlays, or a combination of these modalities. The primary objective is to build computational models that can analyze the videos and determine whether they contain misogynistic content or non-misogynistic content. Participants are encouraged to explore multimodal approaches that leverage audio, text, visual, and contextual information for effective classification. The task seeks to foster innovation in natural language processing, computer vision, speech processing, and multimodal machine learning for low-resource languages. The shared task will provide benchmark datasets, evaluation protocols, and performance metrics to facilitate fair comparison among participating systems. The outcomes of this task are expected to contribute to the development of robust content moderation tools and promote responsible AI solutions for combating online gender-based hate and discrimination in Dravidian language communities.

Objective: Identify videos in Dravidian languages containing misogynistic content

Languages: Malayalam and Tamil

Details: Participants will be provided with videos in Malayalam and Tamil and are required to classify these comments into misogynistic and non-misogynistic. This task requires a deeper level of multimodal understanding and precision.

Language Set Misogynistic Non-misogynistic Total
Malayalam Train 141 162 303
Test - - -
Tamil Train 75 45 120
Test - - -

Starter Kit for Participants

Output Format (Important)

The final submission must be a CSV file containing a column named: prediction.

Save Predictions

Minimal required CSV: only prediction 

submission = df_test[["prediction"]]
submission.to_csv("submission.csv", index=False)
print("Saved: prediction.csv")

Optional (if organizers require Id + prediction): 

# submission = df_test[["Id", "prediction"]]
# submission.to_csv("prediction.csv", index=False)

Authors' Kit for Participation

  • Account Setup:
    • 'Codabench' Account: All participants must create an account on Codabench to participate. This platform will be used for accessing competition details and datasets (click HERE to create an account).
  • Registration Process:
    • Joining the Competition: After logging into Codabench, navigate to the 'My Submission' tab → accept the terms and conditions → select 'Register' to join the competition.
    • Access to Data: Once your registration is approved, you will gain access to the necessary datasets through Codabench in the 'Dataset files' tab.
  • Result Submission:
    • Procedure: Results must be submit via 'Google Form' in 'Result' or 'Evaluation' tab provided by the organizers. Detailed submission guidelines are available in the 'Evaluation' tab.
    • Note: Do not submit your results directly on the Codabench platform.
  • Timeliness: It is crucial to follow the submission deadlines strictly. Late submissions may not be considered for evaluation.

Paper Submission Guidelines

  • Format and Naming Convention:
    • Title Format: The title of the paper should follow this format: TEAM_NAME@MMLow 2026: Title of the paper. For example, XYZ@MMLow 2026: Identifying misogynistic videos in Dravidian languages
    • Authors' Names: Ensure that the authors' names on the paper match those entered in the Google Form during result submission.
  • Length and Content:
    • Document Length: The paper should be a maximum of 4 pages, excluding appendix and citations (references).
    • Paper Template: Paper submissions must use the LaTeX templates.
    • Must include the GitHub link (upload your code and attach that link) in the paper.
    • Note: Poster and demo submissions should be no longer than 4 pages (plus an unlimited number of pages for references and ethics/broader impact statements).
  • References and Citations:
    • Citing the Dataset: Make sure to cite the dataset and any overview papers relevant to the competition. BibTeX entries for these citations will be provided below.
  • Additional Points:
    • Innovation and Originality: Submissions that demonstrate innovative approaches and original research will be highly valued.

  The following are some general guidelines to remember while submitting the working notes.

  • Basic sanity check for grammatical errors and reported results
  • Papers should have sufficient information for reproducing the mentioned results.- Papers should follow the appropriate style.
  • Check the papers for text reuse / plagiarism. This includes self-plagiarism as well. We want to stress this point, as ACL is quite strict about it. Any paper found to have plagiarized content should be rejected without further consideration.
  • Please ensure the author names do not have any salutations like Dr., Prof., etc, in the final version

 All submissions should be in ACL templates below:

  • LaTeX:To be updated
  • Paper submission: To be updated

📧 If you have any queries, contact us 📧

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
Evaluation Criteria

We accept the test prediction results only through the Google Form

Google Form Link

Evaluation Methodology

  • Submissions for this task will be evaluated at the video level using the following metrics: Accuracy, Macro Precision, Macro Recall, Macro F1-score, Weighted Precision, Weighted Recall, and Weighted F1-score.

  • Official Ranking Criterion: Participants will be ranked based on the Macro F1-score.

 

Participants are required to submit the predicted data in a comma-separated value (CSV) file.

Paper submissions should be in the below format.
Overleaf Template:

To be updated

Paper Submission Link:

To be updated

 

The classification system’s performance will be measured in terms of macro-averaged precision, macro-averaged recall, and macro-averaged F1 score across all the classes. Participants are encouraged to check their system with the sklearn classification report

  • https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.metrics.classification_report.html

📧 If you have any queries, contact us 📧  

b_premjith@cb.amrita.edu, g_jyothishlal@cb.amrita.edu, and bharathiraja.akr@gmail.com

pages.title: Dataset files
pages.content:
Datasets

We present the datasets containing YouTube vidoes for the below languages annotated for misogynistic/non-misogynistic.

Dataset Structure The dataset is organized into the following folders:
  • MSGNY_Train_Mal: Contains video files belonging to the Misogyny class.
  • NON_MSGNY_Train_Mal: Contains video files belonging to the Non-Misogyny class.
A separate Excel file (mal_label_train.xlsx) is provided containing the annotations for all videos. The file consists of the following columns:
  • File ID – Unique identifier corresponding to each video file.
  • Label – Class label assigned to the video (Misogynistic or Non-misogynistic).
Participants are encouraged to use the provided annotations to map each video to its corresponding class label during model development and evaluation. Please ensure that the file identifiers are used consistently when processing the dataset. If you encounter any issues with the dataset or annotations, kindly contact the organizers.
Language Train Test (unlabeled) Test (labeled)
